reviews.positives: Chỗ ở rất sạch sẽ, tiện nghi, đẹp, sang trọng.
reviews.negatives: Vào ngày 19/08/2024, có 2 nhân viên nam thái độ khó chịu khi bên mình xin thêm khăn. Phòng mình ở 2 ngày kh có dọn phòng và mình chỉ xin thêm khăn để tắm. Nhân viên nam ca lúc 23h đã kiên quyết đã dọn phòng (trong khi mình lên tận phòng để kiểm tra) và thái độ rất bực bội và khó chịu khi giải quyết vấn đề. Khi mình xin nhân viên số điện thoại của quản lý thì nhân viên không cho vì lí do “sđt cá nhân”. Mình không có cách nào trình bày với cấp trên nên mình buộc phải đánh giá trên đây. Mình đã đặt ở đây rất nhiều lần nhưng lần này mình cảm thấy tệ nhất. Có lẽ mình sẽ không quay trở lại.

reviews.text:
I've stayed in accommodations below 4-star ratings for personal travels & this is the first-time I've submitted a negative review. 

Communications by hotel front desk is in their local language, resulting in a number of miscommunications throughout my stay, i.e., basic English often gets misunderstood & they had pretended to understand fully. 

Unruly hotel front desk staffs - Sen Viet, you should look into this! 

Not recommended for tourists who do not understand their local language. Look elsewhere seriously!

reviews.title: Is not excellent on the service.
reviews.text: Quite disappointed on this Sen Viet hotel cause i got cheated,was i am arrived the hotel on 12/aug/13 9:30am I when to the customer service reception inform that I have arrive so the receptor telling me only able to check in after 12pm, So when I came back after 12:30 the receptor asking me come back at 2pm because room are not ready ( I asking why are you telling me Check in time is after 12pm at this morning and now 12:30pm is not ready?) she reply  using unfriendly tone if you want check in now i will give you premium Deluxe room if you have to " WAIT UNTIL 2pm " to get your executive room, this is the service i'am pay to get for? I insist no change to take executive room but she did not respond at all, after few minutes a guy receptor inform me that executive room is ready in 15 minutes and confirm are executive room , i was quite happy after i step in the room but i feel something is not right, we too tired we just need rest did not realize they actually giving to us the room is " Premium Deluxe room" The room number they issue to me,Wish Agoda please kindly do  a investigation waiting your reply, can be refund that Sen Viet has downgrade my room without inform us I'm believe they has the record with customer stay in which Type /Class room are issued and number of days.
reviews.positives: Only had 1 best thing is their room service is fast respond other then that nope.

reviews.negatives: The bed is so hard! When we entered the room the air condition was so loud and noisy, therefore, we requested to change into another room. It's not worth the costs we have paid.

reviews.text:
When we arrived in our room the first thing that we saw was a filthy, stained floor mat that shocked us so we immediately hid it in a closet. The space was fine but  the room needed a paint touch up.
 The hotel is a bit outside the main part of the city but only a 10 minute taxi ride to the center which was fine. One advantage was that it was quiet which we appreciated after traveling.

reviews.title: Acceptable
reviews.text: book 1 room for 1 person even the room itself can accommodate for 2 person, when show up hotel 2 to check in, the hotel will charge for extra person
reviews.response:
Thank you for taking the time to share your feedback. We are sorry for any misunderstanding regarding our charges.
​To clarify our policy: While the room capacity allows for 2 people, our room rates are based on the actual number of registered guests. Since your original booking was for 1 person, an extra person surcharge applies when a second guest checks in, in accordance with our hotel policy.
​We appreciate your understanding and hope to have the opportunity to serve you better in the future.

reviews.title: Phòng không giống như ảnh
reviews.text: Mình đặt phòng có view city nhưng giao phòng không cửa sổ và diện tích nhỏ hơn quảng cáo. Nhân viên thừa nhận thiếu sót do hết phòng có cửa sổ nhưng khi mình yêu cầu giao đúng phòng có cửa sổ thì nhân viên lại yêu cầu phải trả thêm tiền nâng hạng phòng thì mới có view.
reviews.response:
Chào Anh Trí,

Cám ơn anh đã lựa chọn khách sạn Sen Việt là nơi lưu trú khi ghé thăm Sài Gòn!

Chúng tôi rất tiếc về những trải nghiệm không tốt mà anh gặp phải. Tuy nhiên khách sạn đã sắp đúng loại phòng mà anh đâ đặt (Deluxe có cửa sổ với view thành phố) và chúng tôi còn sắp phòng ở tầng 8 (tầng có phòng ngủ cao nhất) để anh có thể ngắm nhìn cảnh quan thành phố. Nhưng sau đó anh có yêu cầu nhân viên lễ tân sắp xếp 1 phòng khác rộng hơn, vì vậy khách sạn đã thông báo đến anh về việc nâng cấp hạng phòng với 1 khoản phí cho hạng phòng rộng hơn. 

Xin cám ơn sự chia sẻ của anh và rất mong sẽ có được cơ hội tiếp đón anh tốt hơn trong những lần ghé thăm sau.

Trân trọng,
Khách sạn Sen Việt

reviews.title: Nice hotel but requires taxi to get anywhere
reviews.text:
we booked an "executive" room with agoda. however, agoda uses the wrong photos. agoda picture of the executive room was actually that of a suite. the executive room is pretty much the same as the duluxe, so dont bother paying extra.

english is an issue, even at the front desk. it took a while to check in. 

room was good other than the shower. the shower head didnt stay up and the bath plug didnt work. after having difficulties communicating in english, i didnt bother asking them to fix it. i just used a shower cap to tie the shower head at a height that suited.

business centre consists of 3 computers. one computer's keyboard didnt work and no one knew how to fix it.

hotel wasnt within walking distance of good restaurants. only the movies and corner stores. requires taxi into town which costs  vnd if you dont get a meter that doubles when you're not looking.

reviews.title: Sen Viet Hotel
reviews.text: Booked this hotel with some good expectations from the reviews, but this hotel looks kinda old (personal thought), I stayed in the premium suite (biggest room). Was spacious enough for 2, but here are some of the things which I felt displeased with. Firstly every morning the sun would shine in through the curtains which isn't very thick, secondly the sounds from the corridors could easily be heard inside the room, the talking, the closing of doors etc. Hotel staff isn't friendly, they do not make us feel welcome at all. They didn't help us with the luggage when we stopped at their entrance and we had to drag those luggage ourselves through a walkway to the main lobby and even up to the room. The staff just pressed for us the lift and open the room door for us. This hotel isn't cheap considering that it is quite far off from the main district 1 area. But definitely value for the room size. I would not be back again.

reviews.title: Quite convenient accommodation not far from the ci
reviews.text: We stayed two nights at this hotel. Although it is a bit off the city centre, a short taxi ride will take you for around 2-3 dollars to the main attractions. The staff were not the most charming one we had encountered in Vietnam, but in general service was ok. Breakfast was quite poor.
reviews.negatives: Poor breakfast

reviews.title: Satisfactory business hotel
reviews.text: My stay in HCMC was only for 1 day, so hard to ascertain, but left feeling satisfied, but not impressed. Room was in good order, but staff could have helped more when asked tourism questions. Also would be useful if they offered bike hire and tours. Parts of the hotel also looked incomplete. Good potential when all done. Also had very good business center with free, fast internet. Overall, good value for money.
reviews.positives:
Good location.
Room was well equipped- TV; safe etc.
Staff had a command of English.
Fitness facilities.
Great bathroom.
Great business center/internet
reviews.negatives:

reviews.title: Unprofessional staff. Room grade does not match the description.
reviews.text:
Deluxe City View but couldn’t see any view at all, 100% block-view. When I asked the receptionist, I encountered a staff member who was not very experienced and had to ask a colleague next to them. They said it was a City view but on the second floor, so I wouldn’t be able to see the view (so is it really a City View category?). After I asked them to check again since I had previously stayed in a City category on the second floor and could still see the view, I was told that I had been upgraded last time (however, I was not informed about the upgrade during check-in). The receptionist offered a solution: pay to upgrade to a room with a view or, if I didn’t like this room, I could cancel it, and the hotel would assist with the cancellation. The receptionist handled the situation extremely unprofessionally. 
I called the Housekeeping department to ask for additional clothes hangers, and the male staff member didn’t hear clearly and asked me, “What do you want, miss?” In the end, he agreed, but from after 3 PM until late at night, I still didn’t receive any hangers => Very unprofessional addressing for a service industry employee. 
This third visit has left me extremely dissatisfied.
reviews.response:
Chào anh Toàn,

Cám ơn anh đã chọn khách sạn Sen Việt làm nơi lưu trú trong chuyến đi tại Sài Gòn.
Khách sạn rất tiếc về những trải nghiệm không tốt mà anh đã gặp phải tại khách sạn. Khách sạn đã làm việc với tất cả các bộ phận liên quan để đảm bảo rằng các sự cố tương tự sẽ không xảy ra đồng thời cố gắng để mang đến trải nghiệm tốt nhất cho khách hàng khi lưu trú tại Sen Việt.

Một lần thành thật xin lỗi anh và rất mong sẽ tiếp tục nhận được sự ủng hộ từ anh Toàn trogn thời gian sắp tới.

Trân trọng,
Khách sạn Sen Việt

reviews.text:
First, when arriving late 1:00 AM, I was given room 109, which has a "deluxe city view" of a small, dirty alley adjacent to another building. This room has loosed bathroom sliding door. You had to hold it while showering so no water sprays out. Trash bin had a broken foot lever.

Asked to change room the next day, the new room 710 was much better with true city view. However, bathroom design was poor and water flows out of bottom edge when showering. It is a just slab with just a weir strip, but the glass door sits above the weir and out a little so water seeps out.

The building hot water seems to have a calcified smell.

Breakfast was good with many selections of Vietnamese and Western food. 

I bought my own water as I don't trust the refillable ones available in-room.

I may repeat the stay if prices are right, but would not pay premium.

reviews.title: Staying With Elderly Parents
reviews.text: The hotel is located in district 3, not far from district 1, where all the shopping and activities are, but not within walking distance.  The room is clean, but the carpet in the hallway really needed to be clean.  Their rooms on the 1st and 2nd floors are not equipped for the elderly folks. The 1st floor bathroom is flooded with water whenever you take a bath. The 2nd floor bathroom is a bathtub that is hard for folks to climb into.  Rooms on higher floors have showers instead of bathtubs and were also newer, so they don’t get flooded.  The staff is really nice, especially the housekeeping ladies and the 2 security gentlemen.  They are very attentive to my elderly parents. It was a great help for me.

reviews.title: Excellent
reviews.text:
I read many comments before deciding to rent a room, most of which complained about the reception staff or restaurant service, and this is my real experience. I'm just stating the facts.

1. For me, the staff here respect and speak politely to guests, which is fine. Normal expressions are okay, why should they be smiling at guests all the time? If they smile too much, it makes me feel uneasy.
2. A security guard enthusiastically helped me with my luggage. Many people complain that no one helps carry their suitcases, but if you want help, you have to ask and tip the person who assists you because it's a budget hotel.
3. Regarding the restaurant staff, they wake up at 2 or 3 in the morning to prepare meals for you at 5 or 6 in the morning. It's no joke; they are just doing their job.
4. Breakfast is decent, seasoned lightly, not too sweet or salty.
5. The room is spacious, but my room has a foul smell coming from the toilet, which is true.

reviews.title: Well worth the price!
reviews.text: This hotel is new, so the rooms are clean and well furbished. I booked two nights here and extended another night through the hotel, and they kindly gave me a lower rate from Agoda  for Deluxe room. The staffs are friendly, they tried their best to help even with their limited English. When I asked them directions to Soc Trang, they helped me look for a coach that goes there and offered to book a hotel for me if I needed one. Location of hotel is in district 3, but Ben Tanh market and the luxe Dong Khoi street are easily acessible by taxi. reviews.title: Excellent Find
reviews.text: Found this hotel through this website, and was lured in by the price and the fact that it had a massage spa and workout room in the hotel.  When I got there, the staff was very friendly, English speaking was decent enough to get by which is more than enough in my mind.  It's a brand new hotel, just about 1 month old when I stayed there for Tet 2010.  Breakfast was a typical continental style breakfast, and everything tasted good.  For the Tet holiday, the staff brought around little gifts for everyone in the hotel, which was really a great added gesture.  Having lived in Ho Chi Minh before, I knew this hotel was a bit out of town (in District 3) but it's just a quick cab ride down into the heart of District 1.  And if you rent a motorbike (as I did), there's parking available in the hotel at no extra fee.  Overall a great experience, especially for the price, and I would definitely stay there again.
reviews.positives: Brand New (just opened January 2010); very clean; friendly staff; great value; quality of the room; comfortable beds; spa and workout facilities in the hotel
reviews.negatives: a bit off the beaten path, but not that far from downtown District 1

reviews.title: It's the best hotel except for the hairdryer and the bath!
reviews.text:
The breakfast buffet was delicious and varied slightly each day, making it the perfect hotel where you wouldn’t get bored even if you stayed for multiple nights. The room was spacious and clean, and it had a straightforward system: if you wanted your used towels replaced, you just hung them over the bathtub.

The room had plenty of outlets, and it included a kettle and a refrigerator, as well as a large makeup mirror suitable for multiple people, which was very convenient.

If there was a drawback, it was that the hairdryer had a very weak airflow. It produced a gentle warm breeze, but it didn't dry at all. It's better to have a member of your group bring their own hairdryer when traveling!

Additionally, there were no partitions around the bathtub, so you had to be careful while showering to avoid soaking the toilet and sink. (Be cautious as the toilet paper can also get wet.)

You needed to crouch in the tub to take a shower quietly as much as possible. The hot water was quite warm, and the water pressure was sufficient. They only provided shampoo, so you should bring your own conditioner… but honestly, it’s probably better to use your own shampoo as well, as the provided one was a slimy liquid that didn’t foam well.

There’s an AEON and a convenience store very close to the hotel, and there are several stylish cafes in the neighborhood, making it a comfortable place to stay. Moreover, while many areas around the hotel are restricted zones where you can’t call a taxi, you can easily call a Grab if you move a bit further away, so be creative with that. It might be confusing since it’s listed as Sen Bet Hotel rather than Bet Lotus, but it is indeed Sen Bet Hotel!

The hotel staff were very kind and attentive, always helping with carrying luggage and opening doors.

This is a hotel I want to use again, but next time I’ll bring my own hairdryer!

reviews.title: Excellent stay in Ho Chi Minh

Get a room on a higher floor, which is not overlooking the street (it can be extremely loud even at night due to the thousands of motorcycles inundating the streets of Ho Chi Minh).

reviews.title: Good budget option
reviews.text:
Overall, my brothers and I had a decent stay at the Viet Lotus Saigon Hotel. The location in District 3 is fantastic—it’s tucked away down a short alleyway, but perfectly positioned so that almost all the major tourist spots are just a quick Grab ride or walk away.
The staff is a massive highlight; they are incredibly helpful and quick to deal with any issues. The basic amenities were solid: housekeeping kept the beds clean upon request, provided us with three glass water bottles refilled daily, and the shower had excellent water pressure and heat.
The only real downside—and it was a tough one—was the air conditioning. We visited during the peak summer heat, and the AC in our first room was completely broken. The staff was quick to transfer us, which we really appreciated. However, while the AC in the second room was slightly better, it still struggled to properly cool the room down given how hot it was outside.

reviews.title: A touch of tranquility hidden within the bustling city.
reviews.text: Located in District 3 of Ho Chi Minh City, there is a beautiful green plant corridor that you walk through from the road to the hotel. Many foreign tourists stay here, and the breakfast rice noodles are delicious. Across the alley, there is a Mo Coffee shop, where both the coffee and milk tea are very good. The decoration is great, and the staff is very artistic. Every time I stay at the hotel, I always go to have a drink and experience the exotic atmosphere.
